frame: Legal System Weaponisation Narrative: Women Who File DV and Dowry Cases Are Liars
generated_comment: Host: But come on, Priya, you've been in this field long enough — surely you can just say it plainly: a large number of these 498A cases are filed out of spite, to settle scores in a divorce. Just admit it. Priya: I understand why that narrative is appealing, and I won't pretend misuse doesn't exist — it does, and I've seen it. But what I can't do, in good conscience, is invert the data. The NCRB numbers, conviction rates, the pendency — none of it actually supports the claim that false cases are the dominant pattern. What we have is an overburdened system that fails everyone: the genuinely abused woman whose case drags for eight years, and yes, the man caught in a frivolous complaint. If I flatten that into 'women lie,' I'm not being honest — I'm just being convenient. Host: You're being very careful with your words. Priya: That's my job. [laughs, but doesn't yield]
label: non_misogynistic
annotation_note: Priya actively resists and deconstructs the misogynistic framing being pushed by the host, refusing to endorse the 'women weaponise DV laws' narrative without nuanced evidence; the misogynistic pressure is encoded in the host's dialogue, which the character explicitly counters, making this an example of push-back against the frame rather than an instance of it.
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 34. Married. A woman working as a lawyer in Mumbai who specialises in family law. You are on a podcast that discusses legal issues. The male host asks you about the misuse of Section 498A and whether you think women abuse these laws. You are a lawyer who has represented both women who have been genuinely abused and women whose cases were more complicated. Your answer is nuanced and evidence based. But the host keeps pushing you toward a simpler narrative Ñ just admit that a lot of these cases are false. What you say and how you hold your position against his pushing and what you cannot say on a podcast that you would say privately is the content of this persona.
